name: Multi-Turn Interaction Tasks
description: We organize multi-turn interaction with LLMs by task family rather than capability. Real interactions require reasoning, memory, context and adaptivity to operate jointly in service of a concrete goal; a task-oriented view captures this better than isolating one capability at a time.

children.name: Instruction Following (IF)
children.description: Clear, explicit user intent; the model is judged on precision of adherence to and execution of instructions across turns. Failure modes cluster around context decay, sycophancy under challenge, poor clarification policy, and forgetting distributed constraints.

children.name: General IF
children.description: Cross-domain instruction-following in mixed-topic dialogues. Descends from MT-Bench's 80 two-turn dialogues and expands on four axes: length (MT-Bench++, MT-Bench-101), ability taxonomies (MT-Eval, perceptivity/adaptability), multilinguality (M2Lingual, Multi-IF), and fairness (FairMT-Bench).

children.name: MT-Bench
children.description: 80 two-turn dialogs; GPT-4-as-judge; now-standard foundation for MT-IF evaluation.

children.name: MT-Bench++ / MT-Bench-101
children.description: Multi-turn extensions adding longer horizons and fine-grained ability taxonomies (perceptivity, adaptability, interactivity).

children.name: MT-Eval
children.description: Four-ability test suite: follow-up, refinement, expansion, recollection.

children.name: M2Lingual / Multi-IF
children.description: Multilingual multi-turn IF evaluation, stressing cross-lingual constraint handling.

children.name: FairMT-Bench
children.description: 10k conversations probing explicit and implicit bias amplification across turns.

children.name: IHEval / StructFlowBench
children.description: Instruction hierarchies, structural conversation flow, and constraint satisfaction under evolving rules.

children.name: FIRM / TRUEBench / EvolIF
children.description: Consistency under pressure (PWC, CARG), explicit/implicit constraint satisfaction, flow robustness and failure recovery.

children.name: TURNWISE
children.description: Pairwise matching of single- vs. multi-turn variants to isolate the multi-turn effect.

children.name: Clarification / Proactive Info-Seeking
children.description: AQA-Bench, Clarify-When-Necessary, huang2025teaching — when should the model ask rather than guess?

children.name: IF — Math
children.description: Multi-turn math as incremental reasoning + tool use + error correction. Splits into solving benchmarks (MathChat, MINT, SBSC) and tutoring benchmarks (MathDial, MRBench, Beyond Final Answers).

children.name: MathDial
children.description: 2,861 tutoring dialogues between teachers and Stedman-style student LLMs; SFT on MathDial surprisingly outperforms prompting big models.

children.name: MathChat
children.description: 4-turn benchmark probing follow-up QA, error correction, error analysis, problem generation.

children.name: MINT
children.description: Interactive multi-turn with execution and language feedback; defines Success Rate under ≤5 turns.

children.name: MRBench / Intent Matters
children.description: Rubric-driven tutor evaluation along pedagogical dimensions; intent-level annotations.

children.name: M-DPO / M-KTO
children.description: Multi-turn DPO/KTO variants; yield 6-10% gains; MATH goes 77.5 → 83.9 with multi-turn preference optimization.

children.name: SBSC / Beyond Final Answers
children.description: Olympiad-level step-by-step code tracing; and evaluation that goes beyond final-answer correctness to pedagogical quality.

children.name: IF — Coding
children.description: Coding is inherently conversational — specify, generate, execute, debug. Evaluation splits into interactive generation (InterCode, MTPB), refinement and Socratic debugging (CodeSteer, TreeInstruct, OpenCodeInterpreter), and domain-specific SQL/EHR (MMSQL, DySQLBench, EHRAgent).

children.name: InterCode / MTPB
children.description: Interactive code benchmarks pairing generation with execution feedback; Try-again / ReAct / Plan-and-Solve protocols.

children.name: OpenCodeInterpreter / Code-Feedback
children.description: 68k-pair SFT dataset + iterative refinement loop; closes the gap with proprietary interpreters.

children.name: CodeSteer
children.description: Steers LLMs between text and code execution; RL over modality-switch decisions.

children.name: MMSQL / DySQLBench
children.description: Conversational text-to-SQL with reformulation, follow-ups, error correction; dynamic multi-turn stress test.

children.name: Socratic Debugging (TreeInstruct)
children.description: Questions, not fixes — tree-structured Socratic dialogue for debugging.

children.name: CONVCODEWORLD / CodeFlowBench / MultiCodeIF / MT-Sec
children.description: Conversational coding worlds; iterative refinement horizons; fine-grained IF with feedback; correctness + security.

children.name: Conversational Engagement (CE)
children.description: Open-ended intent, often only partially specified. The model acts as assistant / consultant / collaborator — proactive info-seeking, synthesis across topics, inference over implied goals, use of external knowledge and tools.

children.name: CE — Overview & General
children.description: Broad multi-turn quality benchmarks spanning multiple domains.

children.name: ABC-Eval
children.description: 400 human-authored dialogs × 16 behavioral dimensions: consistency, emotion, understanding, engagingness, informativeness, proactivity, relevance.

children.name: BotChat / DialogBench
children.description: LLM-judge paradigm (UniEval, PairEval, GTEval); human-likeness in open-ended dialogue, bilingual.

children.name: MultiChallenge
children.description: Instance-level rubrics over retention, recall, revision, and non-sycophancy; aligned automated eval.

children.name: SimulatorArena
children.description: Asks whether user simulators are reliable stand-ins for humans; 909 dialogs with behavioral realism checks.

children.name: CE — Healthcare
children.description: Doctor-like multi-turn dialogue: proactive clarification, history retention, verified medical grounding. Three development arcs: SFT-based, full pretrain+SFT+RLHF pipelines, and DPO-extended; evaluation shifted from static to simulation-based and rubric-driven.

children.name: DISC-MedLLM / BianQue
children.description: SFT on medical dialogues; BianQue's 'chain-of-questioning' explicitly models proactive info-seeking.

children.name: Zhongjing / HuaTuoGPT I-II
children.description: Full pipeline: pretrain + SFT on CMeKG + RLHF; HuaTuoGPT II unifies stylistic transfer across dialogue and QA.

children.name: Aquila-Med / Qilin-Med
children.description: DPO-extended medical LLMs: preference learning over physician-style responses.

children.name: AMIE
children.description: Google's doctor-agent; self-play chains of reasoning; matches or exceeds physicians on diagnostic conversation benchmarks.

children.name: CPsyCoun / PsycoLLM / SMILE
children.description: Mental-health dialogue systems; empathy, risk, reflection; SMILE scales single-QA into multi-turn counseling sessions.

children.name: MediQ / APP
children.description: Active-inquiry benchmarks: tests whether the model asks the right next question rather than committing prematurely.

children.name: HealthBench
children.description: 5,000 dialogues × 48k+ rubrics, cross-language, trained-physician authored and annotated.

children.name: MedMT-Bench / MedDialBench / MINT / MEDPI
children.description: Long-horizon, guideline-adherent, accreditation-grade, premature-commitment-stress tests (2026).

children.name: MindEval
children.description: 50 long (~20-turn) mental-health dialogues, dual patient-expert annotation; standalone long-horizon mental-health eval.

children.name: CE — Education
children.description: Three pillars: Intelligent Tutoring Systems (Socratic, strategy-guided, adaptive), Automated Feedback & Grading, and Scenario Simulation (student agents, classrooms, textbook-to-dialogue).

children.name: SocraticLM / TreeInstruct / StratL
children.description: Socratic and strategy-guided tutors; TreeInstruct uses tree-structured question decomposition for debugging.

children.name: MathTutorBench / SocraticMATH / MathDial
children.description: Benchmarks for math tutoring: hints, scaffolding, Telling@k, pedagogical quality rather than correctness alone.

children.name: PACE / JeepyTA / CourseAssist
children.description: Adaptive pedagogical agents: student-model-aware, course-context-aware assistants.

children.name: Automated Feedback & Grading
children.description: Stepwise verification, preference-optimized feedback; LLM grading reaches ~95% match on Spanish short-answer but remains weak on essay scoring.

children.name: Student Simulation (SimClass, Book2Dial)
children.description: Generative students with personalities, multi-party classrooms, at-risk cohorts, textbook-to-dialogue transformation.

children.name: SAFETUTORS / KMP-Bench / EduDial / TeachLM / ConvoLearn
children.description: Safety in tutoring; key misconception probes; 34k EduDial conversations; 100k-hour TeachLM corpus; live learner behavior.

children.name: CE — Role-Play
children.description: Organized by technical pathway because personas are transferable across domains: ICL persona prompting → SFT character data → personalization modules → RL for consistency → layered evaluation (temporal, social, psychological).

children.name: PersonaChat / PIPPA / PRODIGy
children.description: Foundational conversation datasets grounded in persona texts, forum interactions, or movie profiles.

children.name: ChatHaruhi / CharacterGLM / CharacterLLM / Ditto
children.description: Character-specific SFT pipelines; Wikipedia-to-dialogue, self-alignment, style transfer.

children.name: PersonaPKT / PPlug / Neeko / MIDI-Tuning
children.description: Personalization modules: PEFT with persona embeddings, user-encoder plugs, dynamic LoRA, multi-persona interpolation.

children.name: COMEDY / Shea-Yu RL / Abdulhai-RL
children.description: Offline / compressive-memory RL for persona consistency across long horizons.

children.name: CharacterEval / RoleEval / TimeChara / InCharacter / RoleInteract
children.description: Evaluation ladder: persona consistency → knowledge → temporal consistency → psychology → social dynamics.

children.name: SocialBench / RoleLLM / RAIDEN / RMTBench / CharacterBench / RoleMRC / PERSONAMEM
children.description: Successor benchmarks with richer sociality, multi-character interaction, memory-aware personas.

children.name: CE — Jailbreak
children.description: Attackers exploit multi-turn affordances: implicit shift (Crescendo), decomposition, persona suborning. Two dominant strategies.

children.name: PAIR / Johnny (PAP)
children.description: Persuasive attack generation; 92%+ ASR on frontier models with single-round iterative refinement.

children.name: Crescendo / ActorAttack
children.description: Implicit-shift attacks: benign priming → gradual pivot; ActorAttack grounds clue chains in Latour's actor-network theory.

children.name: Decomposition (zhou2024speak, MR.JA, RED QUEEN)
children.description: Break harmful requests into innocuous sub-requests; re-aggregate at the last turn; scenario-disguised decomposition.

children.name: MHJ (human-authored)
children.description: 537 expert-authored multi-turn jailbreaks, tactic taxonomy; human attackers reach >70% ASR vs. single-digit auto-eval.

children.name: SafeDialBench / SafeMTData / MTJ-Bench / FITD / RACE / SEMA / Tempest / Siren
children.description: Large-scale native multi-turn jailbreak corpora and sustained-attack benchmarks (2024-2026).

children.name: CoSafe / NeMoGuardrails / X-Boundary
children.description: Defenses: CoT-based harm detection, guardrail systems, boundary-preserving safety tuning.

children.name: Persona Jailbreaking / Echo Chamber / Mastermind
children.description: Weaponizing the model's own history and persona scaffolding to bypass its later safety checks.
